title: Im With Cupid

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's signature exclamation after some mishap
jokes.punchline: The iconic 'D'oh!' followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfectly economical character expression that became cultural touchstone

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: And then the handsome prince realized... he had to go to the bathroom really bad. But the evil ogre- Barney- had left the men's room in the most wicked condition.
jokes.setup: Homer telling what seems like a fairy tale to the kids
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a mundane story about needing the bathroom at Moe's
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that subverts fairy tale expectations with Homer's typical lowbrow reality

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Once upon a time, there was a brave knight who ventured into a dark and mysterious tavern...

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, is this going somewhere?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: He searched desperately through winding corridors, growing more and more desperate, when suddenly...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: He found the bathroom at Moe's. The end.

dialogue.speaker: Maggie
dialogue.line: *sucks pacifier*

jokes.text: That's not a fairy tale. It's just something that happened to you at Moe's.
jokes.setup: Homer's attempted fairy tale
jokes.punchline: Lisa's deadpan correction exposing the reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - intelligent, cutting through Homer's nonsense with precision

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Once upon a time, there was a beautiful princess who lived in a castle...

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, that's not a fairy tale, that's just a description of your life savings getting spent on Duff beer.

jokes.text: And thank you, God, for the bad things adults do... which distract attention from stuff I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Bart saying grace at dinner
jokes.punchline: Reveals his cynical gratitude for adult dysfunction as cover
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive prayer that perfectly captures Bart's mischievous intelligence

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dear God, we thank you for this food. We're also grateful for all the things that make life worth living: beer, TV, and the crippling inability of adults to communicate their feelings, which means nobody will ever find out about the time I...

jokes.text: Thanks. It's due tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for help with school project
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals it's due tomorrow after getting agreement
jokes.explanation: Classic setup revealing procrastination - good structure but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey Dad, can you help me with my school project?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sure, son. When's it due?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Tomorrow.

jokes.text: All right. I'll get you the materials. - But you're building it! - Okay. Oh, I'll make the whole thing!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to set boundaries about helping with project
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation to doing the entire project herself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Marge's enabling parenting pattern

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart, I can help you with your project, but you need to do most of the work yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Come on, Mom, just a little help?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: No, Bart. You're capable of doing this.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Mom...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Okay, fine, I'll just help with the poster board.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Great! And the research?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I suppose I could organize your notes...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: And maybe type it up?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, your handwriting is so messy...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: And build the model?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I've already started, actually.

jokes.text: But this is the peak hour for stoned teenagers buying shiny things.
jokes.setup: Someone expressing surprise the Kwik-E-Mart is open at 11:30 PM
jokes.punchline: Apu's matter-of-fact business analysis of late-night customers
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor delivered with Apu's businessman pragmatism

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the night shift customer is a different breed. He comes in at 11:30 PM, purchases a single item—perhaps a frozen burrito or a magazine of questionable content—and leaves without making eye contact. It is a beautiful symbiosis.

jokes.text: Whoa! It's a living mirror. Cool hat!
jokes.setup: Apu's comment about stoned teenagers buying shiny things
jokes.punchline: Immediate appearance of stoned teen mesmerized by shiny surface
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback that validates Apu's observation

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Stoned teenagers always buy shiny things.

dialogue.speaker: Stoned teenager
dialogue.line: Whoa... shiny...

jokes.text: I told her that endless toil is the only true path out of this jerkwater burg.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if working long hours is rough on Apu's marriage
jokes.punchline: Apu's romantic justification for neglecting his wife
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Apu's self-deception about his work obsession

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Rough? No, no. I tell my wife, 'Manjula, I work hard so that one day we can afford the things we have always dreamed of.' She says, 'Apu, we have eight children.' I say, 'Exactly! Eight children, Manjula! Each one requiring shoes!'

jokes.text: It is payback time, and this time it's personal.
jokes.setup: Apu talking about having Homer and Marge over for dinner
jokes.punchline: Action movie cliché applied to dinner invitation
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor but the reference is predictable

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I am inviting Homer and Marge over for dinner. It will be a dinner to remember.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But first, they must get past my state-of-the-art security system.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Security system? Apu, it's just dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, but if they cannot survive the dinner preparations, they are not worthy of my table!

jokes.text: I didn't turn it on yet. [Gurgling Continues] Oh.! I'm never eatin' chili again. Ooh, chili!
jokes.setup: Digestive system project making realistic sounds
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's Homer's actual digestive system, followed by his contradictory chili reaction
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment - perfect character contradiction and physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, check out my digestive system project! It makes all the authentic sounds.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's just your actual digestive system.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, I had some chili for lunch... or did I? I can't remember, but my stomach sure can!

jokes.text: I'm solving world hunger.
jokes.setup: Nelson wrecking something
jokes.punchline: His grandiose justification for destructive behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - combining his bullying nature with absurd self-importance

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ha-ha!

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: I'm not destroying things out of malice. I'm a craftsman, perfecting my art. Each broken window is a brushstroke, each dented car a sculpture. I'm not a bully—I'm a misunderstood artist!

jokes.text: Very. But I can't help wondering where I go from here.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's happy about causing destruction
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis after achieving destructive goal
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - unexpected depth for Nelson with perfect deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ha-ha!

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Wait... now what?

jokes.text: [Gasps] Seven? I'll be right there.
jokes.setup: Apu checking on his store's penny count
jokes.punchline: Treating seven pennies as an emergency requiring immediate attention
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Apu's obsessive business management

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Seven pennies?! This is a catastrophe! I must call the Federal Reserve at once!

jokes.text: If you need pennies, take them from Jerry's jar.
jokes.setup: Apu obsessing over penny shortage
jokes.punchline: Manjula's casual dismissal with mysterious Jerry reference
jokes.explanation: Decent beat showing marital dynamics and mysterious Jerry detail

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: A penny shortage? Apu, who cares about pennies? Even Jerry doesn't care about pennies anymore.

jokes.text: I could not let you in the store like that because you are smoking.
jokes.setup: Apu complimenting Manjula's outfit
jokes.punchline: 'Smoking' as both attractive and literally smoking hot
jokes.explanation: Standard pun - functional but not particularly clever or original

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: My goodness, Manjula, you look absolutely smoking hot in that outfit!

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: Thank you, Apu!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: No, I mean you are literally on fire.

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: Well, let us just say we both feel bad. - Deal.
jokes.setup: Escalating politeness about each other's homes
jokes.punchline: Mutual agreement to feel ashamed ending the politeness spiral
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution to politeness escalation with practical compromise

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Your Kwik-E-Mart is so clean and well-organized!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Oh, thank you! But your home is so warm and inviting!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's sweet, but your store has such efficient inventory management!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: You are too kind! Your living room curtains are absolutely exquisite!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, your Squishee machine brings joy to so many children!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Your basement is a marvel of organization and—

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Okay, we should probably feel a little ashamed of ourselves.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, agreed. This is ridiculous.

jokes.text: Good rice, good curry, good Gandhi, let's hurry.
jokes.setup: Homer eating Indian food
jokes.punchline: Rhyming praise that inappropriately includes Gandhi
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cultural insensitivity delivered with innocent enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmmm... this curry is so good! It's spicy, it's tangy, it's got that perfect kick! This is the best Indian food I've ever had! It's almost as great as... Gandhi!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, why are you comparing food to Gandhi?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Because it's spicy like Gandhi, and it makes me happy like Gandhi, and it's brown like Gandhi!

jokes.text: [Snickers] Oh, yeah. Homer's a real go-getter.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how hard it is to get people away from work
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic take on Homer's work ethic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's rare sarcasm about Homer's laziness

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Yeah, well good luck getting Homer away from work. He's so dedicated to that power plant, he'd probably keep showing up even if they paid him in donuts.

jokes.text: What's an 'eltdown'?
jokes.setup: Homer's pager going off
jokes.punchline: Homer misreading 'MELTDOWN' on his pager display
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stupidity moment - missing the first letter of nuclear emergency

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh my God, a message from work! Let me see... M-E-L-T-D-O-W-N... Meltdown? Oh, that's not a word I usually see on my pager...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, wait... Could it be... MEAT DOWN? Like, the meat is down? That sounds like a sale!

jokes.text: Eighteen hours? Nobody works that hard.
jokes.setup: Apu claiming he works 18-hour days
jokes.punchline: Marge and Apu's shocked reaction to this being considered normal
jokes.explanation: Decent culture clash humor about American vs immigrant work ethics

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I work 18-hour days.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: What? This is normal for a convenience store owner.

jokes.text: Week-ends?
jokes.setup: Learning Homer gets weekends off
jokes.punchline: Apu's confusion at the concept of weekends
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word captures Apu's immigrant work mentality

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Weekends? What is this 'weekends' you speak of?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, Saturday and Sunday. Days off.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Days... off? I do not understand this concept. The Kwik-E-Mart is open 24 hours, 365 days a year. There are no 'days off.' I have not slept since 1987.

jokes.text: You told me it was an American tradition... to work all the time and never see your wife!
jokes.setup: Apu's excuses for working constantly
jokes.punchline: Manjula revealing Apu's ridiculous justification for neglect
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of Apu's self-serving cultural misrepresentation

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: You are always working! What is your excuse this time?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Manjula, I work hard to provide for our family. The store needs me.

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: That is what you said last night, and the night before, and every night for eight years!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But the Kwik-E-Mart cannot run itself.

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: You know what Apu told me? He said he has to work because if he does not, squirrels will take over the store!

jokes.text: 'Sala' seems to mean 'jerk'... and I think 'Manjula' means some kind of spaceship.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to understand their foreign language argument
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong translations including thinking wife's name means spaceship
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer ignorance - confident misunderstanding with absurd spaceship conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, I understand what you're saying. Marge means spaceship, Bart means slingshot, and Lisa means saxophone.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, those aren't even close to what we're saying!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, you're speaking some kind of weird language. How am I supposed to know?

jokes.text: Oh, they're newlyweds. They'll just talk things out and top it off with some lovin'.
jokes.setup: Marge worrying they caused trouble
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive optimism about marital conflict resolution
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer obliviousness - functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I'm worried we caused real trouble by arguing in front of the kids.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, don't worry. I'm sure the kids didn't even notice. They were too busy watching TV.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, they were watching us argue!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: See? Problem solved. As long as we keep them entertained, they'll never know we're fighting.

jokes.text: Either put that book down, or let me drive!
jokes.setup: Homer reading while driving
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated but logical demand
jokes.explanation: Decent setup showing Homer's dangerous multitasking and Marge's patience

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, eyes on the road!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Marge, I'm reading the manual. It says here the car practically drives itself.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's the owner's manual, Homer. For the car, not for you!

jokes.text: 'You're a-peeling. Let's never split!' [Laughing] That's funny, 'cause they're monkeys.
jokes.setup: Homer reading Valentine's card
jokes.punchline: Explaining the banana pun with characteristic literal-mindedness
jokes.explanation: Homer over-explaining simple pun is character-appropriate but weak joke

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, a Valentine's card! Let me read this... 'You're the top banana in my bunch.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, did you know I'm a banana? Because I'm yellow and I'm shaped like a banana.

jokes.text: Morning, Apu. Still in hot water with the squaw?
jokes.setup: Homer greeting Apu after the dinner incident
jokes.punchline: Casually offensive term showing Homer's cultural insensitivity
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorance played for cringe but relies on offensive term

jokes.text: That would be like going to an air show and leaving before the plane crash.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Manjula won't leave before Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Dark comparison treating relationship drama like disaster spectacle
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor with good structure - unexpected but logical comparison

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: She's not going anywhere before Valentine's Day. That's ratings gold!

jokes.text: 'Tax Hike Approved'? Bad dog. Bad, bad dog.
jokes.setup: Homer making dog fetch newspaper
jokes.punchline: Blaming dog for bad news content
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - blaming messenger for message with perfect absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Bad dog! Bad dog! Why would you bring me news about the stock market crash?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This is all your fault, Santa's Little Helper. You're ruining my day with these terrible headlines.

jokes.text: I'd love to look at 'Scullery Week' if you're through with it.
jokes.setup: Homer hoarding newspaper sections
jokes.punchline: Marge's interest in mundane domestic magazine
jokes.explanation: Good Marge characterization - finding interest in domestic topics

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, why do you have all these newspaper sections scattered around?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm saving them, Marge. You never know when you might need the classified ads or the TV guide.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, while you're hoarding those, I'll be reading my new magazine about better ways to organize the linen closet.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That sounds boring.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Says the man who keeps newspapers from 1987.

jokes.text: This is about investment opportunities in Yemen. - Give me that!
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing section as uninteresting
jokes.punchline: Marge's sudden intense interest in Yemen investments
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - Marge showing unexpected interest in exotic finance

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yemen? That's not interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Wait, Yemen? I'm very interested in Yemen investments!

jokes.text: 'Successful mayor type seeks open-minded discreet cheerleader type.'
jokes.setup: Reading personal ads
jokes.punchline: Obvious reference to powerful official seeking inappropriate relationship
jokes.explanation: Standard political satire - obvious but functional

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Listen to this one: 'Powerful public official seeks young woman for discreet relationship.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Eh, sounds like Mayor Quimby.

jokes.text: The only ad you took out was to sell our lawn mower.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he used to take out romantic ads
jokes.punchline: Marge's deflating correction about his actual advertising history
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's false romantic history with practical reality

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I used to take out romantic ads in the personals.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: You took out an ad that said 'Lazy, good-for-nothing dad seeks…' and I answered it.

jokes.text: Well, sometimes I find pickle slices in the sheets.
jokes.setup: Being asked if Homer has covered bed with flowers
jokes.punchline: Marge's sad substitute - finding food debris in bed
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - perfectly captures Homer's slob nature and Marge's resigned acceptance

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, he didn't cover the bed with flowers. He covered it with potato chip crumbs, pretzel fragments, and what I think might be a chicken wing.

jokes.text: Oh! Just once I'd like a parrot to say that to me.
jokes.setup: Hearing about romantic limo invitation from trained bird
jokes.punchline: Marge's wistful desire for parrot romance
jokes.explanation: Great Marge moment - specific, sad desire showing her romantic deprivation

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: A trained parrot? How romantic... I wish Homer would do something like that for me.

jokes.text: It's not that monkey card I found in the car, is it?
jokes.setup: Homer promising special Valentine's surprise
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific knowledge of his low-effort backup plan
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Marge knows Homer's patterns and low standards

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, what's this special Valentine's surprise you promised me?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's a secret, Marge. You're gonna love it.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, is it the thing where you show up at the last minute with gas station flowers and a half-eaten box of chocolates?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: How did you—?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Because that's what you do every year.

jokes.text: Help.! I can't breathe.!
jokes.setup: Apu appearing as chocolate husband
jokes.punchline: Romantic gesture becomes suffocation hazard
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - romantic gesture gone wrong with genuine peril

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: My beloved, I have made myself entirely of chocolate for you!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Apu, that's so romantic!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Now embrace me, my love!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Wait... I'm getting chocolate all over my face... I can't breathe... there's chocolate in my nose!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Yes, let the love smother you!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: No, Apu, I'm literally suffocating!

jokes.text: Sarah, please! Sarah, it's $10 a pill.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to get romantic response from date
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's using expensive performance drugs
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character moment - desperation and cheap drug revelation

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: So what do you say, beautiful? How about we get outta here and go back to my place?

dialogue.speaker: Date
dialogue.line: I don't know... you seem a little too eager.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Eager? That ain't me, baby. That's the four Viagra I took before you got here.

jokes.text: Everybody's marriage is falling apart except ours.
jokes.setup: News report about marriage counseling increase
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's smug satisfaction about others' problems
jokes.explanation: Good Lovejoy character moment - judgmental satisfaction in others' misery

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Oh, people are getting divorced? Well, I'm not surprised. Marriage is a sacred institution, and when people don't follow God's word, well... *chuckles* ...that's what they get. More counseling sessions means more collection plate donations. I mean, more souls to save. Yes, souls.

jokes.text: Look! The Taco Bell dog! Yo quiero- Hey, where'd it go?
jokes.setup: Homer examining temporary tattoo
jokes.punchline: Tattoo disappearing while he's trying to show it off
jokes.explanation: Decent physical gag but relies on dated pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, check out my temporary tattoo! It's a skull with flames!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, where'd it go? It was right here a second ago!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, temporary tattoos only last a few days.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A few days?! But I just got it!

jokes.text: Edna won't even let me clap her erasers.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Valentine's pressure affecting relationships
jokes.punchline: Innocent school activity with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Decent double entendre that fits Skinner's awkward character

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Valentine's Day puts so much pressure on relationships. It's unrealistic expectations, expensive gifts, and forced romance.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour
dialogue.line: Why, just this morning I was planning a special evening with Mrs. Krabappel, but the pressure got to me.

dialogue.speaker: Edna
dialogue.line: Oh Seymour, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, I thought we could exchange... valentines at school. You know, a nice innocent activity.

dialogue.speaker: Edna
dialogue.line: That sounds lovely.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes, we'll have the children make heart-shaped boxes, practice their stroking techniques, and work those fingers until they're nice and supple.

jokes.text: My Barbara will no longer pleasure me with the French arts!
jokes.setup: Complaining about Valentine's pressure
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pretentious description of sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - over-elaborate language for basic concepts

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: The sexual experience of a nerd is a sad and pathetic thing. However, I have discovered a way to make it less depressing. I have named my penis 'The Squishy' and I have instructed it to think of itself as the hero in an adventure story. That way, when I am alone with a woman, I can pretend that my penis is on a noble quest.

jokes.text: The gal I'm stalkin' had me bumped back to 200 feet.
jokes.setup: Complaining about relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions stalking and restraining order
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Moe humor - treating serious crime as minor inconvenience

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Yeah, my relationship's on the rocks. She got a restraining order against me.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: I don't even know what I did wrong. I was just following her to work, checking on her at night...

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Apparently that's 'stalking' now. Since when is that illegal?

jokes.text: Well, I guess we are. I suppose I do take Maude for granted.
jokes.setup: Being asked who's to blame for relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Ned's immediate self-blame and reflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned - immediate guilt and self-reflection while others blame external forces

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Well, I'd have to say it's me. I'm too judgmental, too preachy, and I don't listen well enough. Plus, I'm always trying to save people, which comes across as condescending.

jokes.text: It's easy to blame ourselves... but it's even easier to blame Apu!
jokes.setup: Ned's self-reflection and responsibility
jokes.punchline: Moe redirecting to scapegoat with twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Great Moe logic - avoiding responsibility with perverse reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Yeah, well that's all fine and good Flanders, but let me tell ya something. You know who's really responsible for all this? That dang Homer Simpson! If he wasn't always causin' trouble, none of us would be in this mess in the first place!

jokes.text: Right after happy hour!
jokes.setup: Declaring they must stop Apu immediately
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing drinking over their righteous mission
jokes.explanation: Good group character moment - priorities revealing true nature

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: We gotta stop Apu immediately!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Yeah, let's go right now!

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: This is our righteous mission!

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Right after we finish our beers.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Good call.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Yeah, no point going thirsty.

jokes.text: Gasoline, axes. I got some stuff in the trunk.
jokes.setup: Homer asking how to sabotage romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Moe's casual mention of serious weapons
jokes.explanation: Good Moe darkness - casual mention of violence for minor problem

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey Moe, how do I sabotage a romantic gesture?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Well, you could use C-4, a couple of land mines, maybe a rocket launcher.

jokes.text: Not yet I'm not! You'll know when I'm driving! And it'll- [Screams]
jokes.setup: Being told he's driving
jokes.punchline: Homer's declaration followed by immediate crash
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Homer's overconfidence immediately undermined

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm the best driver in the world!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *crash sound*

jokes.text: Only till 11:00.
jokes.setup: Comment about having breakfast at Tiffany's
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's literal interpretation of movie reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent Wiggum moment - missing cultural reference with helpful correction

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Breakfast at Tiffany's? I had a cup of coffee and a donut at Tiffany's jewelry store.

jokes.text: They'll keep my men from resorting to homosexuality... for about 10 minutes.
jokes.setup: Receiving magazines for his crew
jokes.punchline: Time estimate for how long entertainment will work
jokes.explanation: Classic Sea Captain - nautical stereotypes with crude humor

dialogue.speaker: Sea Captain
dialogue.line: Ah, fresh magazines for the men. Let's see what we have here... 'National Geographic,' 'Popular Mechanics,' 'Penthouse'... Excellent! This should keep them entertained for... oh, a good three minutes.

jokes.text: We had to make an emergency landing. It's that damn chandelier again.
jokes.setup: Elton John appearing at Springfield airport
jokes.punchline: Blaming his flamboyant stage prop for flight problems
jokes.explanation: Great celebrity parody - logical explanation for elaborate stage presence

dialogue.speaker: Air Traffic Controller
dialogue.line: We're going to need you to remove that candelabra from the aircraft.

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: But it's part of my act!

dialogue.speaker: Air Traffic Controller
dialogue.line: I don't care if it's part of your act. It's a fire hazard.

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: This is discrimination against the flamboyantly challenged!

jokes.text: You know, when I was dubbed Sir Elton, the queen paddled me silly.
jokes.setup: Being put in cage as part of fake knighthood
jokes.punchline: Suggesting real knighthood involved kinky activity
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity humor - subverting royal ceremony with sexual suggestion

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: Wait, you mean real knighthood involves this?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Uh, yeah. That's what 'knight' means.

jokes.text: I am so sick of that damn code!
jokes.setup: Skywriter explaining professional obligation
jokes.punchline: Homer's exaggerated frustration with skywriting ethics
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment - treating minor obstacle as major irritation

dialogue.speaker: Skywriter
dialogue.line: I have a professional obligation to write what the customer requests.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But it says 'Buy Zesta'! I can't read that high!

jokes.text: I love you- It's an angel.! Oh, that's Neddie's pet name for me.
jokes.setup: Sky-written love messages appearing
jokes.punchline: Maude interpreting garbled message as personal
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment of misinterpreting accident as romance

dialogue.speaker: Maude
dialogue.line: Oh, a love message written in the sky! How romantic!

dialogue.speaker: Maude
dialogue.line: It says... 'Eat at Joe's'? No, wait... 'I love you, Maude'!

dialogue.speaker: Maude
dialogue.line: Heavens to Betsy, it's for me!

jokes.text: Blobbie? Upsilon?
jokes.setup: Trying to read garbled skywriting message
jokes.punchline: Marge's confused attempts to interpret mangled text
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag showing communication breakdown

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What does it say? 'Eat at Joe's'? 'Beat up Joe's'? 'Eat up Joe's'?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It says 'See Dr. Marvin Monroe.'

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, that's silly. We don't need to see a doctor.

jokes.text: Oh, who am I kidding? Homer would never surprise me like that.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to believe Homer sent romantic skywriting
jokes.punchline: Her realistic assessment of Homer's romantic capabilities
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Marge's sad but realistic self-awareness about her marriage

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, how romantic! Homer must have sent this skywriting just for me!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Although... knowing Homer, it probably just says 'Donuts' or 'Free Beer'.

jokes.text: Well, well, the bitch is back.
jokes.setup: Elton John responding to situation
jokes.punchline: Reference to his own song in context
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity self-reference - using his own song title as commentary

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: Well, I'm still standing, yeah yeah yeah.

jokes.text: You see, because that is your song. I heard you. Yes, because someone saved your life tonight.
jokes.setup: Homer making obvious song references
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining Elton John song titles to Elton John
jokes.explanation: Homer's clueless explanation of songs to their composer

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey Elton, I love your song 'Rocket Man.' It's about a man who is a rocket!

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: Yes, well, it's actually a metaphor for—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And 'Tiny Dancer'! That's about a dancer who is tiny!

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: If you'd just let me—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And 'Goodbye Yellow Brick Road'! You're saying goodbye to a road that is yellow and made of bricks!

dialogue.speaker: Elton John
dialogue.line: I'm leaving.

jokes.text: Just when I think I have you figured out, you fall from the sky with roses.
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally delivering roses after plane crash
jokes.punchline: Marge interpreting accident as romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Excellent romantic comedy resolution - Marge's tendency to find meaning in Homer's accidents creates perfect ironic romance

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, you shouldn't have! Flowers after that terrible plane crash? You're so thoughtful!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Plane crash? What plane crash?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: The one you were just in! You crashed and somehow got me these beautiful roses!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, that. I didn't get you those on purpose.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: You didn't? But Homer, that's the most romantic thing you've ever done!

jokes.text: Well, I think I have a collapsed lung, but okay.
jokes.setup: Marge promising romantic reward for gesture
jokes.punchline: Homer's medical concern vs romantic priorities
jokes.explanation: Good Homer priorities - serious injury vs sex consideration

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: If you do this for me, I'll make it worth your while tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You mean...?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But what about my crippling back pain?

jokes.text: Well, you and the health inspector. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Manjula surprised he closed store for her
jokes.punchline: Revealing dual motivation for romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu - deflating romantic moment with practical business reality

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: You closed the Kwik-E-Mart for me? I can't believe it!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Of course, my love. Anything for you.

dialogue.speaker: Manjula
dialogue.line: This is the most romantic thing you've ever done!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Well, actually, the health inspector is coming tomorrow, and I needed time to hide the rats.

jokes.text: It should get you pretty darned hammered.
jokes.setup: Offering champagne Squishee
jokes.punchline: Casual description of alcohol's effect in convenience store context
jokes.explanation: Good Apu moment - matter-of-fact approach to romantic alcohol

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Thank you, come again!

tmdb_overview: Apu is making the husbands of Springfield look bad with his extravagent Valentine's Day efforts for Manjula. When the men try to sabotage Apu's grand gesture, they accidentally wind up benefiting from it.
insight: Valentine's Day romance plot dilutes joke density to 2.89/min, lowest-scoring Season 10 entry.
